title: Callipteris matamensis
taxonomy: Callipteris matamensis is classified as a species within the genus Callipteris. The genus Callipteris belongs to the family Matoniaaceae. This family is part of the order Polypodiales. The order Polypodiales is a subgroup within the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is a division of the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies.

intro: Callipteris matamensis, also known as Matamoros fern, is a type of plant species belonging to the family Matoniaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Callipteris matamensis include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its health benefits. Herbal preparations made with Callipteris matamensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
